 {"id":"bd-bkul","title":"Simplify wisp architecture: single DB with ephemeral flag","description":"
## Problem

The current wisp architecture uses a separate .beads-wisp/ directory with its own database. This creates unnecessary complexity:

- Separate directory structure and database initialization
- Parallel routing logic in both beads and gastown
- Merge queries from two stores for inbox/list operations
- All this for what is essentially a boolean flag

## Current State

Gastown mail code has to:
- resolveWispDir() vs resolveBeadsDir()
- Query both, merge results
- Route deletes to correct store

## Proposed Solution

Single database with an ephemeral boolean field on Issue. Behavior:
- bd create --ephemeral sets the flag
- JSONL export SKIPS ephemeral issues (they never sync)
- bd list shows all by default, --ephemeral / --persistent filters
- bd mol squash clears the flag (promotes to permanent, now exports)
- bd wisp gc deletes old ephemeral issues from the db
- No separate directory, no separate routing

## Migration

1. Update beads to support ephemeral flag in main db
2. Update bd wisp commands to work with flag instead of separate dir
3. Update gastown mail to use simple --ephemeral flag (remove all dual-routing)
4. Deprecate .beads-wisp/ directory pattern

## Acceptance Criteria

- Single database for all issues (ephemeral and persistent)
- ephemeral field on Issue type
- JSONL export skips ephemeral issues
- bd create --ephemeral works
- bd mol squash promotes ephemeral to persistent
- Gastown mail uses simple flag, no dual-routing
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T20:06:27.980055-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T20:43:07.065124-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T20:43:07.065124-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: single DB with Wisp flag, deprecated wisp storage functions"}
+{"id":"bd-blh0","title":"gt nudge doesn't work with crew addresses","description":"## Bug

`gt nudge beads/crew/dave "message"` fails because it uses the polecat session manager which produces wrong session names.

## Expected
Session name: `gt-beads-crew-dave` (hyphen)

## Actual
Session name: `gt-beads-crew/dave` (slash, from polecat manager)

## Root Cause

In nudge.go line 46-57:
```go
rigName, polecatName, err := parseAddress(target)  // beads, crew/dave
sessionName := mgr.SessionName(polecatName)        // gt-beads-crew/dave (WRONG)
```

Should detect `crew/` prefix in polecatName and use `crewSessionName(rigName, crewName)` instead.

## Fix

```go
if strings.HasPrefix(polecatName, "crew/") {
    crewName := strings.TrimPrefix(polecatName, "crew/")
    sessionName = crewSessionName(rigName, crewName)
} else {
    sessionName = mgr.SessionName(polecatName)
}
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:43:32.222784-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T15:43:32.222784-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bqcc","title":"Consolidate maintenance commands into bd doctor --fix","description":"Per rsnodgrass in GH#692:
> "The biggest improvement to beads from an ergonomics perspective would be to prune down commands. We have a lot of 'maintenance' commands that probably should just be folded into 'bd doctor --fix' automatically."

Current maintenance commands that could be consolidated:
- clean - Clean up temporary git merge artifacts
- cleanup - Delete closed issues and prune expired tombstones
- compact - Compact old closed issues
- detect-pollution - Detect and clean test issues
- migrate-* (5 commands) - Various migration utilities
- repair-deps - Fix orphaned dependency references
- validate - Database health checks

Proposal:
1. Make `bd doctor` the single entry point for health checks
2. Add `bd doctor --fix` to auto-fix common issues
3. Deprecate (but keep working) individual commands
4. Add `bd doctor --all` for comprehensive maintenance

This would reduce cognitive load for users - they just need to remember 'bd doctor'.

Note: This is higher impact but also higher risk - needs careful design to avoid breaking existing workflows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/capable","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:31.466556-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.732363-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.732363-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}
 {"id":"bd-bw6","title":"Fix G104 errors unhandled in internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go:1181","description":"Linting issue: G104: Errors unhandled (gosec) at internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go:1181:4. Error: rows.Close()","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T15:35:09.008444133-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-bwk2","title":"Centralize error handling patterns in storage layer","description":"80+ instances of inconsistent error handling across sqlite.go with mix of %w, %v, and no wrapping.

Location: internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go (throughout)

Problem:
- Some use fmt.Errorf("op failed: %w", err) - correct wrapping
- Some use fmt.Errorf("op failed: %v", err) - loses error chain
- Some return err directly - no context
- Hard to debug production issues
- Can't distinguish error types

Solution: Create internal/storage/sqlite/errors.go:
- Define sentinel errors (ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidID, etc.)
- Create wrapDBError(op string, err error) helper
- Convert sql.ErrNoRows to ErrNotFound
- Always wrap with operation context

Impact: Lost error context; inconsistent messages; hard to debug

Effort: 5-7 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:54.974909-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:44:37.237175-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:44:37.237175-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: errors.go exists with sentinel errors (ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidID, ErrConflict, ErrCycle), wrapDBError/wrapDBErrorf helpers that convert sql.ErrNoRows to ErrNotFound, and IsNotFound/IsConflict/IsCycle checkers. 41 uses of wrapDBError, 347 uses of proper %w wrapping, 0 uses of %v. Added one minor fix to CheckpointWAL."}

 {"id":"bd-kwro.7","title":"Identity Configuration","description":"Implement identity system for sender field.

Configuration sources (in priority order):
1. --identity flag on commands
2. BEADS_IDENTITY environment variable
3. .beads/config.json: {"identity": "worker-name"}
4. Default: git user.name or hostname

New config file support:
- .beads/config.json for per-repo settings
- identity field for messaging

Helper function:
- GetIdentity() string - resolves identity from sources

Update bd mail send to use GetIdentity() for sender field.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T03:02:17.603608-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-kwro.8","title":"Hooks System","description":"Implement hook system for extensibility.

Hook directory: .beads/hooks/
Hook files (executable scripts):
- on_create - runs after bd create
- on_update - runs after bd update  
- on_close - runs after bd close
- on_message - runs after bd mail send

Hook invocation:
- Pass issue ID as first argument
- Pass event type as second argument
- Pass JSON issue data on stdin
- Run asynchronously (dont block command)

Example hook (GGT notification):
  #!/bin/bash
  gt notify --event=$2 --issue=$1

This allows GGT to register notification handlers without Beads knowing about GGT.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T03:02:23.086393-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-kwro.9","title":"Cleanup: --ephemeral flag","description":"Update bd cleanup to handle ephemeral issues.

New flag:
- bd cleanup --ephemeral - deletes all CLOSED issues with ephemeral=true

Behavior:
- Only deletes if status=closed AND ephemeral=true
- Respects --dry-run flag
- Reports count of deleted ephemeral issues

This allows swarm cleanup to remove transient messages without affecting permanent issues.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T03:02:28.563871-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"bd-kyll","title":"Add daemon-side delete operation tests","description":"Follow-up epic for PR #626: Add comprehensive test coverage for delete operations at the daemon/RPC layer. PR #626 successfully added storage layer tests but identified gaps in daemon-side delete operations and RPC integration testing.

## Scope
Tests needed for:
1. deleteViaDaemon (cmd/bd/delete.go:21) - RPC client-side deletion command
2. Daemon RPC delete handler - Server-side deletion via daemon
3. createTombstone wrapper (cmd/bd/delete.go:335) - Tombstone creation wrapper
4. deleteIssue wrapper (cmd/bd/delete.go:349) - Direct deletion wrapper

## Coverage targets
- Delete via RPC daemon (both success and error paths)
- Cascade deletion through daemon
- Force deletion through daemon
- Dry-run mode validation
- Tombstone creation and verification
- Error handling and edge cases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:08:26.039663309-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:44:03.584007-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T01:44:03.584007-08:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed"}

 {"id":"bd-kzda","title":"Implement conditional bond type for mol bond","description":"The mol bond command accepts 'conditional' as a bond type but doesn't implement any conditional-specific behavior. It currently behaves identically to 'parallel'.

**Expected behavior:**
Conditional bonds should mean 'B runs only if A fails' per the help text (mol.go:318).

**Implementation needed:**
- Add failure-condition dependency handling
- Possibly new dependency type or status-based blocking
- Update bondProtoProto, bondProtoMol, bondMolMol to handle conditional

**Alternative:**
Remove 'conditional' from valid bond types until implemented.

This is new functionality, not a regression.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/toast","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:23:01.966367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.734264-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.734264-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}
